landing.title: Early avian outbreak intelligence for veterinarians.
landing.subtitle: Querencia helps field vets capture observations, triage disease risk, and escalate outbreaks without exposing farms.
landing.heroDescription: The platform turns confidential veterinary reports and official signals into disease briefs, regional risk views, and network response workflows.
landing.coveredSpecies: The operating product is avian-first because that is where the current veterinary workflow is active.
landing.operatingModelDescription: Field vets see the earliest signs, but escalation is often fragmented, delayed, or too exposed. Querencia structures that moment into a private, reviewable signal.
landing.workflowDescription: The product is organized around one field-ready loop: report, review, regionalize, and respond.
landing.workflowStepOne: A verified veterinarian submits a confidential avian signal with field evidence.
landing.workflowStepTwo: The system places the report in disease context and keeps source confidence visible.
landing.workflowStepThree: Regional aggregation and review protect farm identity while surfacing risk.
landing.workflowStepFour: The network receives prioritized alerts, briefs, and weekly follow-up outputs.
landing.privacyBody: Farm identities never appear in the regional layer. Sharing stays safe enough for real veterinary use.
landing.validationBody: Reports are not treated as generic incidents. They are evaluated inside an avian disease surveillance workflow.
landing.coordinationBody: The product helps veterinarians move from local observation to shared response without losing context.
landing.sourcePolicyBody: Querencia combines verified veterinary reports with official confirmed signals. Open public submissions do not drive the system.
landing.publicContextDescription: Regional views combine verified veterinary input and official sources into a privacy-safe avian intelligence surface.
landing.publicContextNote: Farm identities stay private. Public-facing context remains aggregated and policy-controlled.
landing.whyDescription: Avian outbreaks are first recognized in the field, but veterinarians often work with fragmented escalation, weak decision support, and privacy-sensitive reporting paths.
landing.challengeOneBody: Important observations start in calls, notes, or spreadsheets instead of a structured surveillance workflow.
landing.challengeTwoBody: A field signal needs disease context, regional context, and confidence state before a network can act on it.
landing.challengeThreeBody: Farm-level exposure makes people hesitate. Regional aggregation preserves confidentiality while still surfacing risk.
landing.scienceDescription: Querencia combines field evidence, disease-specific reasoning, regional aggregation, and outbreak trend modeling into one veterinary surveillance loop.
landing.scienceOneBody: Reports capture observed disease, evidence summary, case estimate, and region so the signal is useful immediately.
landing.scienceTwoBody: Avian disease briefs translate clinical and epidemiological knowledge into practical triage support for veterinarians.
landing.scienceThreeBody: Signals are aggregated to regions and mapped into a surveillance layer that highlights where activity is intensifying.
landing.scienceFourBody: The same signal can feed briefs, alerts, trend views, and weekly summaries for veterinary networks.
landing.demoDescription: The demo stays focused on three connected surfaces: report intake, disease intelligence, and network response.
landing.demoReportBody: A veterinarian can submit an avian signal with evidence, region, and case estimate in one field-ready workflow.
landing.demoIntelBody: Disease pages combine practical guidance, surveillance framing, and regional context for avian conditions.
landing.demoConsoleBody: Dashboards and response boards show how field reports become risk views, alerts, and follow-up coordination.
landing.impactDescription: The goal is earlier escalation, safer sharing, and faster veterinary coordination when avian disease risk begins to move.
landing.impactOneBody: Field observations become structured signals faster, so veterinary networks can react before spread widens.
landing.impactTwoBody: Regional aggregation protects farm identity while still allowing shared surveillance and response planning.
landing.impactThreeBody: The same platform supports clinicians, network leads, and operational reviewers without splitting the workflow.

animals.bovineDescription: Reference disease briefs for veterinarians. No confidential bovine workflow is active in this demo.
animals.porcineDescription: Reference disease briefs for veterinarians. No confidential porcine workflow is active in this demo.
animals.avianDescription: Operational avian disease briefs, private reporting, and regional surveillance built for veterinarians.

diseases.operationalBannerBody: Use these avian briefs as disease context while the private workflow handles confidential veterinary intake, regional risk review, and network response.
diseases.referenceBannerBody: Querencia is not currently running confidential bovine or porcine workflows. These pages remain disease reference surfaces for veterinarians.

diseaseDetail.timelineNoData: No visible series for this disease yet.
diseaseDetail.timelineSubtitle: Observed public series by period so the page shows volume, active regions, and recent intensity instead of an empty block.
diseaseDetail.publicSummarySubtitle: This public layer summarizes source mix, latest visible period, and highest-activity regions using the same seed data that drives the map and KPIs.
diseaseDetail.publicNoRegionalData: No visible regions for this disease yet.
diseaseDetail.delayedPublicRelease: Public release may be delayed or suppressed in low-volume regions.
diseaseDetail.operationalBannerBody: Avian signals from official data and verified veterinarians feed a confidential veterinary workflow first. Regional views remain delayed, aggregated, and policy-controlled.
diseaseDetail.referenceBannerBody: This disease page is informational. It does not represent an active confidential workflow in Querencia today.

account.accessSupport: Operational access is granted through veterinary networks and coordinators.

reporting.title: Report Avian Signal
reporting.subtitle: Confidential intake for verified veterinarians. Turn a field observation into a private surveillance signal with evidence, region, and case context.
reporting.guideDisease: Choose the disease that best matches the observation so the report lands in the right surveillance context.
reporting.guideEvidence: A short evidence note is often enough: lab result, mortality pattern, visit findings, or suspected exposure path.
reporting.guideRegion: Pick the strongest regional match available. The system protects farm identity by aggregating to the regional layer.
reporting.nextOne: The report stays confidential and keeps its veterinary source context.
reporting.nextTwo: Regional review determines whether the signal should affect shared surveillance and alerting.
reporting.nextThree: Validated signals can feed disease briefs, trend views, and weekly network follow-up.
reporting.visibilityPrivate: Private — only you can see this report
reporting.visibilityPublic: Public — visible in public aggregated views
reporting.evidenceHint: Lab result, veterinary visit, mortality pattern, or other supporting evidence
reporting.avianOnly: Confidential avian intake for verified veterinarians in the field.
reporting.confidentialNotice: Reports remain confidential, preserve source context, and feed regional surveillance only after review.
reporting.pending: Your signal is now in coordinator review.
reporting.error: Could not submit report. Please try again.
reporting.requiresVerifiedVeterinarian: Only verified veterinarians can submit operational signals.
reporting.signInRequired: Please sign in to submit reports.

alerts.title: Network Alert Center
alerts.subtitle: Watch regions and alert coverage for avian coordinators and network members.
alerts.empty: No alerts yet. Set up watch regions to receive avian network alerts.
alerts.avianPilotOnly: Operational watch regions are limited to the avian pilot in v1.
alerts.signInRequired: Please sign in to access alerts.
alerts.maxReached: Maximum watch regions reached for your plan.

monitoring.title: Response Board
monitoring.subtitle: Track watched regions, alert coverage, and follow-up coordination when veterinary signals need a network response.
monitoring.alertsSubtitle: Review triggered events, protected regional coverage, and the areas the network keeps under follow-up.
monitoring.coordinationSubtitle: Organize response portfolios and internal follow-up when a signal needs a deeper veterinary coordination layer.

dashboard.title: Live Demo
dashboard.subtitle: Show how field reports, disease intelligence, regional risk, and model-guided triage fit together in one avian workflow.
dashboard.observedSubtitle: Look at what actually happened first: signal volume, active regions, and recent intensity by disease.
dashboard.chartSubtitle: Historical cases by period. Taller bars mean more confirmed or validated network cases.
dashboard.noData: No avian trend data available for this period.

workspace.title: Response Board
workspace.subtitle: Organize watch regions into response portfolios so veterinarians and network leads can monitor follow-up responsibility and weekly review coverage.
workspace.noPortfolios: No portfolios yet. Create one to organize your watch regions.
workspace.noWatchAreas: No watch regions assigned to this portfolio.

proDashboard.title: Network Triage Snapshot
proDashboard.subtitle: An avian-only operational view for veterinarian leads reviewing hotspots, signal velocity, and weekly reporting priorities.

evolution.title: Risk projection
evolution.subtitle: Predictive contagion evolution model based on real-time data to anticipate which regions may worsen next and where to act first.
